# Intact Insurance — Leadership Interview Pack
> Director AI, Chief Data Office. Leadership and strategy round with Indhira Mani (CDO) and Ankur Gupta (Director Data & ML Products).

## Anchor Sentence
Repeat this throughout. It connects every part of your CV to every line of the job description.

> "My focus is turning AI capability into measurable business outcomes in regulated environments."

---

## Final Round: Indhira Mani and Beccy Brown, Thursday 9 July

> [!tip] This is genuinely the final stage. Ray confirmed progression directly. Treat this as a closing conversation, not a fresh evaluation.

### Who Beccy Brown Is

Vice President, Change and Technology Delivery for Specialty Lines, reporting to Ollie Holden, CIO for UK and Ireland. She leads all CIO-side change and IT delivery across the UK and EU Specialty Lines portfolio. She joined from Aviva after seven years there, most recently as COO for GCS, with prior roles as Chief of Staff and Head of Distribution.

She sits on the CIO side of the business, not the CDO side. Her presence in this final round makes the meeting deliberately cross-functional: Indhira represents vision and data strategy, Beccy represents delivery reality and technology change execution.

### Why She Is in This Meeting

Sachin revealed in the prior session that **Specialty Lines transformation is just starting** and Commercial Lines transformation is entering a new phase. Beccy owns exactly the Specialty Lines programme that is beginning now. Her role in this conversation is almost certainly to assess whether the AI function can integrate with a live, active change and delivery programme, not just design capability in the abstract.

### The Anchor for This Round: The Two Group CEO Goals

Sachin confirmed the group CEO, Charles, has set two goals for the entire organisation:
1. Become the number one commercial insurance provider
2. Grow specialty lines

Every initiative is expected to trace back to one of these two. Use this explicitly as the framing device for any strategic question in this round.

### The Hook for Beccy Specifically

"I understand Specialty Lines transformation is just starting and Commercial Lines transformation is entering a new phase. That is exactly the moment an AI function needs to be embedded from the start, working alongside your change and delivery programme, rather than being retrofitted once the transformation is already underway."

### Question to Ask Beccy

"As Specialty Lines transformation gets underway, where do you see the biggest opportunity for AI to support the change programme directly, and where do you see the biggest risk of AI initiatives running ahead of what the delivery organisation can actually absorb?"

This shows awareness of delivery capacity constraints, not just AI ambition, and speaks directly to her operational and delivery background.

---

## Read This First

This role is **70% leadership, strategy, governance, and stakeholder management**. Only 30% technical. Do not lead with LangGraph, RAG, vector databases, or model benchmarks. Mention them only if asked.

What they are hiring: a Director of AI Strategy, an AI CoE leader, an operating model owner, a governance leader, an executive influencer. Not a Head Data Scientist.

Your strength: you have already been doing this at L&G and EY. Your risk: convincing them you are already at Director level.

---

## The Two People in the Room

### Indhira Mani — CDO, Your Hiring Manager
- People-first and pragmatic. Cares about adoption over architecture purity.
- Her 12-month success metric: the business saying "can you do more of that."
- Believes data is an offensive growth strategy, not a back-office function.
- How to win her: lead with people and business outcomes. Talk about embedding with business teams, not building in a data tower.

### Ankur Gupta — Director Data & ML Products, Your Peer
- Owns the platform you will build on.
- Assessing whether you are technically credible and whether you will complement his team or compete with it.
- How to win him: show the difference between platform engineering and applied AI. He builds the engine, you build what runs on it.

---

## Your 2-Minute Opening

> [!tip] Practise out loud until it runs under 90 seconds. This sets the tone for everything.

"I'm a mathematician by background, with a PhD and around eight years across academia, financial services, and now consulting.

Over the last few years I've moved from building models myself to leading teams and shaping AI strategy, governance, and enterprise delivery.

At Legal and General I built and led a 10-person AI team, owned a £1M budget, delivered over £1.4M of realised value, and designed the enterprise AI operating model covering governance, deployment, and responsible AI.

Today at EY I'm leading agentic AI delivery for energy and insurance clients on Databricks and Azure AI Foundry, helping organisations move from experimentation into production.

What excites me about Intact is the chance to build an AI capability at enterprise scale. The role combines strategy, governance, operating models, and business transformation. This is where I have been operating, not where I am heading."

---

## The Three Frameworks to Know Cold
